steps.summary: concept-rag knowledge base not directly relevant (general books/concepts). Internal codebase research conducted instead: examined existing universal skills (orchestrate-workflow: 163 lines, 18 rules; execute-activity: 137 lines, 7 rules), skill schema structure, workflow rule duplication patterns.
MCP best practices 2026: dynamic loading over monolithic payloads (30-50% context waste), namespace organization for skills, workflow-aligned tool design. Validates start_session slimming approach.

Key synthesis: (1) P1 rules can MERGE into existing skills (not new skills needed), (2) session-protocol must stay in start_session (bootstrap dependency), (3) ~29 guardrail rules stay in rules.toon, (4) 3-phase migration: extract→remove dupes→slim start_session, (5) no server code changes needed for rules.toon slimming.

6 research-phase assumptions identified. 4 code-analyzable: merge into orchestrate-workflow (A-04-01 validated), merge into execute-activity (A-04-02 validated), session-protocol bootstrap dependency (A-04-03 validated), no code changes for slimming (A-04-04 validated). 2 open: prism skill scoping (A-04-05), guardrail vs skill model (A-04-06).
04-kb-research.md created with 8 findings, synthesis with 5 recommendations, 3 open questions for plan-prepare.
Assumptions log updated: total 15 (9 validated, 6 open). Research added 6 new assumptions (A-04-01 through A-04-06).
Convergence in 1 iteration. 4/4 code-analyzable validated. 2 open non-code-resolvable.

keyFindings.mergeNotCreate: P1 extractions can merge into existing orchestrate-workflow and execute-activity skills — no new skill creation needed for P1
keyFindings.bootstrapDependency: session-protocol (11 rules) must stay in start_session — agents need token protocol before calling get_skills
keyFindings.noCodeChanges: Slimming rules.toon requires only file edits, not code changes — rules-loader reads whatever is in the file
keyFindings.migrationOrder: Extract skills → remove workflow dupes → slim start_session (architecture changes last)
keyFindings.guardrailsRemain: ~29 generic behavioral rules stay in rules.toon as upfront guardrails
